highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 delivers managed-code performance optimizations and Skia m151 engine updates.
highlights_body: Allocation-free hot paths in text shaping and color parsing reduce GC pressure in render-heavy apps. The bundled Skia engine tracks Chrome m151 with upstream bug fixes, and several managed APIs gain cleaner signatures.

bullets.detail: Aligns the bundled renderer with Chrome milestone 151, giving consumers the latest upstream Skia engine while preserving the managed API surface.
Two rounds of upstream Skia m150/m151 stability fixes are incorporated, covering rendering correctness and platform-specific issues tracked by the Chrome team.
Drops the duplicate SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM define from the Skia submodule so Emscripten builds no longer emit macro redefinition warnings.
Hex colour parsing is optimised and new ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads are added, removing the need to allocate a string when parsing colours from slices.

bullets.lead: GRContextOptions no longer requires an unsafe context
bullets.detail: The unnecessary unsafe modifier is removed from GRContextOptions, so consuming code no longer needs to opt into unsafe compilation just to configure the GPU context.
SKShaper now reads glyph results through zero-copy spans instead of allocating intermediate arrays, reducing GC pressure in text layout loops.
The four-byte tag parser now uses stack memory instead of a temporary char[4] array, eliminating a small but frequent allocation on hot font code paths.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Removed the redundant SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM define from the Skia submodule to fix WebAssembly build warnings, and contributed a cross-backend golden-image test harness

preview_summaries.4.151.0-p1: Preview 1 opened the 4.151.0 line with the Skia m151 engine update and the WebAssembly build cleanup.
preview_summaries.4.151.0-p2: Preview 2 added allocation-free hot paths in text shaping and colour parsing, SKColor ReadOnlySpan overloads, the GRContextOptions unsafe cleanup, and further upstream Skia m151 stability fixes.

harfbuzz_summary: Makes two HarfBuzzSharp hot paths allocation-free: Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled UTF-8 encoder instead of allocating, and Tag.Parse eliminates the per-call char[4] array.
